jokes.text: It's not a tapestry; it's a travesty.
jokes.setup: Previous character's malapropism
jokes.punchline: Correction that reveals the original error
jokes.explanation: Standard correction humor that completes the wordplay setup

jokes.text: You expecting somebody? No. The Easter Bunny? Baby New Year? The Great Pumpkin? Ho, ho, ho.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if they're expecting company
jokes.punchline: Lists holiday characters that would be impossible/wrong season
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor based on seasonal inappropriateness

jokes.text: You know, I once had a maternity suit like that.
jokes.setup: Character commenting on someone's Santa outfit
jokes.punchline: Implies they were pregnant and wore similar red outfit
jokes.explanation: Visual comparison joke with mild personal overshare

jokes.text: If I'm late with the presents, they'll start opening up each other.
jokes.setup: Going to give presents to juvenile delinquents
jokes.punchline: Dark twist on 'opening presents' - implies violence
jokes.explanation: Clever dark wordplay that subverts innocent Christmas imagery with violent undertone

jokes.text: At 3:15 this afternoon, it struck me that I really wanted to see you. At that same moment, I knew that you were thinking that you wanted to see me. Now, was I right, Alex? Is there a God?
jokes.setup: Phyllis claims divine intervention about wanting to see Alex
jokes.punchline: Equates whether Alex was thinking of her with proof of God's existence
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific delusion that escalates personal narcissism to theological proportions

jokes.text: Well, I think a lot of things all the time. I mean, uh, it's possible I might have been thinking about you, but...
jokes.setup: Phyllis's dramatic question about divine intervention
jokes.punchline: Alex's deflating, practical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that deflates Phyllis's grandiosity

jokes.text: our daughter is out of the country, and my other ex-husband recently won a court order that forbids me to get within 400 yards of him.
jokes.setup: Phyllis explaining why she has no one for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of restraining order
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that reveals disturbing information casually, perfect for Phyllis's oblivious character

jokes.text: Phyllis, I'm not going to ask you what happened last Christmas. You're not? I swear to God I'm not. Well, good, because I'm not about to tear open a wound that healed after a year.
jokes.setup: Alex deliberately avoiding asking about last Christmas
jokes.punchline: Phyllis immediately volunteers the information anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic setup where character claims they won't overshare then immediately overshares

jokes.text: Can I come along with you? No. Alex, I won't bother your fares. I'll lie on the floor. They won't even know I'm there. No! In the trunk, then. Just leave it open a crack so I can breathe.
jokes.setup: Phyllis asking to come along in taxi
jokes.punchline: Escalates to increasingly desperate and absurd proposals
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation sequence showing Phyllis's desperation through increasingly absurd proposals

jokes.text: Okay, but just a crack. No, what am I talking about? You can't come with me.
jokes.setup: Phyllis's trunk proposal
jokes.punchline: Alex briefly considers it before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Good beat showing how Phyllis's desperation makes even Alex consider absurd options

jokes.text: Let me at least stay here until midnight so my doorman will think that I had someplace to go.
jokes.setup: Phyllis being pathetic about Christmas
jokes.punchline: Reveals she wants to fool her doorman about having plans
jokes.explanation: Pathetic character moment that's funny because of how desperately she wants to appear to have a social life

jokes.text: It's Christmas Eve. What the hell? I'm going to let you go home early. Go on. Get out of here. Merry Christmas. Louie, I get off in six minutes.
jokes.setup: Louie being generous letting someone off early on Christmas Eve
jokes.punchline: The person was getting off in 6 minutes anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie moment - fake generosity that costs him nothing

jokes.text: Why? Why, Alex? This is the second year in a row I've let them choose where to spend Christmas and they picked their father. Now I'm wondering if they love him more than they love me. Why? Why, Alex? Well, doesn't he have that great, uh, ski chalet in Aspen?
jokes.setup: Elaine wondering why her kids chose their father
jokes.punchline: Alex points out the obvious material advantage
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observational humor about obvious answer to rhetorical question

jokes.text: ♪ Grab your coat and get your hat, Elaine ♪ ♪ Leave your worries by the doorstep, Elaine ♪ ♪ Life can be so sweet, Elaine ♪ ♪ On the sunny side of the ♪ ♪ Sunny side of the ♪ ♪ Sunny side of the street, Elaine ♪ ♪ Cheer up for the holidays and have a merry Christmas ♪ ♪ Elaine. ♪
jokes.setup: Jim sent Elaine a singing telegram
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top musical performance
jokes.explanation: Standard singing telegram gag - more about the performance than clever writing

jokes.text: I hope that little wink doesn't mean you're going to do something like that for me. Why? Because I would feel very embarrassed at everybody staring at me while some Phillip Morris boy came in here and tap-danced and sang at me.
jokes.setup: Jim winking after sending Elaine a telegram
jokes.punchline: Alex's specific embarrassment about being sung to
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's discomfort with public attention

jokes.text: You've got nothing to worry about, Alex. I would never send a Phillip Morris boy for a guy like you.
jokes.setup: Alex's concern about getting a singing telegram
jokes.punchline: Jim's backhanded reassurance implying Alex deserves worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent backhanded insult disguised as reassurance

jokes.text: ♪ Deck the halls with boughs of holly ♪ ♪ Alex Reiger should be jolly ♪ ♪ That's why Jim has sent me here ♪ ♪ Take it from a dancing fruitcake ♪ ♪ You should be happy 'cause you're such a ♪ ♪ Cute cake. ♪
jokes.setup: Jim's revenge telegram for Alex
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing performance with weak rhymes
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Alex's fear about being embarrassed - the cringe is the joke

jokes.text: ♪ Take it from a dancing fruitcake ♪ ♪ You should be happy 'cause you're such a ♪ ♪ Cute cake. ♪
jokes.setup: Singing telegram continues with Christmas theme
jokes.punchline: Self-referential 'fruitcake' pun leading to 'cute cake' rhyme for Alex
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with fruitcake/cute cake, but somewhat forced rhyme. Visual gag of performance continues.

jokes.text: On behalf of myself and the Sunshine Cab Company, on this Christmas Eve, I just want to say... watch out for drunk drivers and don't pick up anybody who might puke in your cab. Noel, Noel, joy to the world. And get your butts out of here.
jokes.setup: Louie giving a Christmas Eve speech to drivers
jokes.punchline: Completely unsentimenal practical advice followed by sarcastic holiday wishes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - subverts Christmas speech expectations with crass practicality

jokes.text: I didn't say they had to work. I just said, if they didn't work, they might not get cabs with the luxuries that they've grown accustomed to... like reverse.
jokes.setup: Simka accusing Louie of making cabbies work Christmas Eve
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'luxury' is basic car function of reverse gear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where 'luxury' is revealed to be essential basic function - great Louie cheapness

jokes.text: I don't think I'll be able to cash a check made out to 'The Bloodsucking Jackal.'
jokes.setup: Simka writes Louie a check
jokes.punchline: She wrote his name as an insult
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag based on Simka's creative insult

jokes.text: Really? Why? Who would question it?
jokes.setup: Louie says he can't cash a check to 'Bloodsucking Jackal'
jokes.punchline: Simka genuinely wonders who would question that description
jokes.explanation: Perfect innocent deadpan - Simka thinks it's such an accurate description that banks wouldn't question it.

jokes.text: Louie... Louie, you poor, pathetic thing. You only wanted money because you thought you weren't going to be invited. Oh, Louie. Oh, Louie, Louie, Louie. Poor Louie.
jokes.setup: Realizing Louie's motivation for charging for radio use
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic pity that embarrasses Louie
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor about Louie's loneliness being exposed

jokes.text: Do you like Christmas ham with, uh, honey glaze and pineapples on it? Yeah. Good. Then you better bring one.
jokes.setup: Asking if someone likes Christmas ham
jokes.punchline: Twist that they need to bring it themselves
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke about party planning expectations

jokes.text: Did you eat all that?! You know, I eat when I'm depressed. I ate everything in the refrigerator.
jokes.setup: Alex seeing empty food containers
jokes.punchline: Phyllis casually admitting to stress-eating everything
jokes.explanation: Standard depression eating joke with visual component

jokes.text: Did you know that Frosty the Snowman melted in the end?
jokes.setup: Phyllis updating Alex on her Christmas Eve
jokes.punchline: Depressing take on innocent Christmas special
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phyllis finding sadness in children's entertainment

jokes.text: Can I get you anything? A gallon of Rocky Road and cable TV.
jokes.setup: Alex offering to get Phyllis something
jokes.punchline: She requests depression comfort items
jokes.explanation: Efficient character humor about depression coping mechanisms

jokes.text: You want to go to a party? Did you invite me to a party? Or did I just have a hypoglycemic rush?
jokes.setup: Alex inviting Phyllis to party
jokes.punchline: Phyllis wondering if it's a medical hallucination
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical explanation for good news fitting Phyllis's pessimistic worldview

jokes.text: Every Christmas my family would get a-a big, beautiful, Scotch pine tree. We'd gather around and watch the servants decorate it.
jokes.setup: Jim reminiscing about Christmas traditions
jokes.punchline: Reveals privileged upbringing where they watched servants work
jokes.explanation: Good character humor revealing Jim's oblivious privilege

jokes.text: Not there, you fool! Higher and to the left!
jokes.setup: Jim offered to help decorate tree
jokes.punchline: Immediately acts like entitled boss to whoever is decorating
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Jim can't help but revert to entitled behavior

jokes.text: So the guy leaves the cab, and I say, 'Wait a minute. You forgot your gift,' and he says, 'No, no, no, you keep it. You deserve it more than she does.'
jokes.setup: Elaine's story about passenger whose wife was cheating
jokes.punchline: Dark irony that she deserves the gift more than the cheating wife
jokes.explanation: Dark ironic humor about relationship betrayal

jokes.text: A pair of panties. They say, 'Property of Hell's Angels.' Big spender. A four-dollar item. Six-fifty.
jokes.setup: Everyone guessing what's in Elaine's gift
jokes.punchline: Louie's crude and specific guess with price corrections
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie crudeness with oddly specific knowledge of lingerie pricing

jokes.text: Alex, do you have to call me your ex-wife? Oh, excuse me. You remember Phyllis, my podiatrist?
jokes.setup: Phyllis objects to being called ex-wife
jokes.punchline: Alex's absurd alternative introduction
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - calling her podiatrist is somehow less intimate than ex-wife

jokes.text: And since I have none of those, I'm glad to be here.
jokes.setup: Host saying holidays are for family, friends and joy
jokes.punchline: Phyllis matter-of-factly stating she has none of those things
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where Phyllis kills holiday sentiment with brutal honesty

jokes.text: A party of 49 and me. Not one of those 49 people asked me to join them.
jokes.setup: Phyllis went to restaurant to be around people
jokes.punchline: Reveals the pathetic specificity of her isolation
jokes.explanation: Painfully specific details that make her isolation more vivid and uncomfortable

jokes.text: By a bus, I hope.
jokes.setup: Phyllis saying she was 'struck' by a revelation
jokes.punchline: Louie hoping for literal bus strike
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Louie interjection with maximum economy - three words kill Phyllis's dramatic moment

jokes.text: I am a lonely, middle-aged woman unable to deal with Christmas.
jokes.setup: Phyllis's dramatic revelation build-up
jokes.punchline: Devastatingly blunt self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest moment that's funny because of how matter-of-fact it is after dramatic build-up

jokes.text: Who invited Alex's podiatrist?
jokes.setup: Awkward silence after Phyllis's depressing revelation
jokes.punchline: Louie referencing Alex's absurd introduction
jokes.explanation: Great callback that breaks tension while showing Louie's lack of filter

jokes.text: Now, you guys steal anything, I break your heads. Say Merry Christmas. Hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey. I said say Merry Christmas. Say it! And God bless you. Say it!
jokes.setup: Louie introducing juvenile delinquents to the party
jokes.punchline: Threatens them to be cheerful and religious
jokes.explanation: Classic irony of forcing Christmas cheer through intimidation

jokes.text: Oh, and in our country, it is a tradition that the oldest woman leads it. Oh, no. Oh, great; just great. When were you born? Uh, February, 1952. February what? Fourth.
jokes.setup: Need oldest woman to lead prayer
jokes.punchline: Phyllis being forced to reveal her age and lead prayer
jokes.explanation: Standard setup forcing unwanted responsibility on most inappropriate person

jokes.text: Dear Lord, no offense, but, uh, could you spend less time with the people that don't need it? I mean, like the women from the Ice Capades and ballplayers. I mean, Lord, what do you care if a guy's stranded on second base?
jokes.setup: Phyllis leading Christmas prayer
jokes.punchline: Bargaining with God about resource allocation and sports metaphors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character prayer that treats God like middle management while missing point of baseball metaphor

jokes.text: Dear Lord, no offense, but, uh, could you spend less time with the people that don't need it? I mean, like the women from the Ice Capades and ballplayers. I mean, Lord, what do you care if a guy's stranded on second base?
jokes.setup: Phyllis begins Christmas prayer
jokes.punchline: Suggests God wastes time on Ice Capades performers and baseball players
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific prayer - Phyllis's practical theology about divine priorities. Oddly specific examples.

jokes.text: Phyllis, you're nudging God.
jokes.setup: Phyllis gives God practical advice in her prayer
jokes.punchline: Alex points out she's essentially nagging the Almighty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - perfectly captures Phyllis's pushy nature even toward God. 'Nudging God' is perfectly chosen phrase.

jokes.text: Also, for me, Lord, stop short skirts and bring back two refills per prescription on Valium.
jokes.setup: Phyllis continuing her prayer
jokes.punchline: Personal requests about skirt lengths and drug policy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific prayer requests revealing Phyllis's personal hang-ups

jokes.text: Nice of you to throw that in, Phyllis.
jokes.setup: Phyllis finally adding 'peace on earth' to her selfish prayer
jokes.punchline: Alex's sarcastic acknowledgment of the afterthought
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man commentary on Phyllis's self-centered prayer

jokes.text: If I could die now, I'd be a happy man.
jokes.setup: Jim responds to Phyllis's depressing prayer
jokes.punchline: Says he'd be happy to die after that experience
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to Phyllis's depression. Economical but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: You can be as depressing as you want. It won't hurt, because I've got money.
jokes.setup: Phyllis worrying she's ruining Christmas
jokes.punchline: Jim's solution is that money protects him from her depression
jokes.explanation: Perfectly oblivious Jim moment showing his belief that wealth insulates from human problems

jokes.text: I didn't know what to pick, so I told 'em to send everything.
jokes.setup: Massive arrival of entertainment/performers
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual explanation of excess
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate excess showing Jim's approach to problem-solving with money

jokes.text: It's my Christmas gift to you. Uh-huh. So, how do you like your present? Like it? L-Like it? I love it. It's perfect. How-How did you know? Well, it was either that, or a shirt.
jokes.setup: Phyllis giving Alex the 'gift' of leaving for New Year's
jokes.punchline: Alex's enthusiastic gratitude and Phyllis's casual alternative
jokes.explanation: Great ironic gift exchange where absence is better than presence, with Alex's obvious relief

jokes.text: It's my Christmas gift to you. / Uh-huh. / So, how do you like your present? / Like it? L-Like it? I love it. It's perfect. How-How did you know? / Well, it was either that, or a shirt.
jokes.setup: Phyllis says leaving town is her gift to Alex
jokes.punchline: Alex's enthusiastic response, then Phyllis's casual shirt alternative
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing their relationship dynamic - Alex's relief at her leaving, Phyllis's casual consideration.

jokes.text: Come on, let's just pretend, you know... I don't want... I don't want to... Alex, come on, we'll just pretend it's New Year's Eve. Okay? Right here. No, we don't have to do this. Four... Phyllis... Three... Oh, come on, Phyllis. Two... Please, please. One. Why don't you just make it a nice... ♪ Should auld acquaintance ♪ Phyllis, Phyllis, stop that. ♪ Be forgot ♪ Phyllis, Phyllis, stop that. I'm not gonna do it, Phyllis.
jokes.setup: Phyllis wanting a New Year's kiss before leaving
jokes.punchline: Awkward countdown with Alex's increasing resistance
jokes.explanation: Extended cringe sequence showing relationship dynamic through forced romantic gesture
